Question 1 — From Equations to Matrix Form (10 Points)

Consider the following system of four equations in four unknowns:

2x1−x2+0x3+3x4=7−4x1+5x2+x3−2x4=−6x1+2x2+4x3+0x4=100x1−3x2+2x3+x4=1\begin{aligned} 2x_1 - x_2 + 0x_3 + 3x_4 &= 7 \\ -4x_1 + 5x_2 + x_3 - 2x_4 &= -6 \\ x_1 + 2x_2 + 4x_3 + 0x_4 &= 10 \\ 0x_1 - 3x_2 + 2x_3 + x_4 &= 1 \end{aligned} 2x1​−x2​+0x3​+3x4​−4x1​+5x2​+x3​−2x4​x1​+2x2​+4x3​+0x4​0x1​−3x2​+2x3​+x4​​=7=−6=10=1​

  1. Write the system in matrix form Ax=b\mathbf{A}\mathbf{x}=\mathbf{b}Ax=b.
  2. Clearly identify A\mathbf{A}A, x\mathbf{x}x, and b\mathbf{b}b.
  3. State the dimensions of A\mathbf{A}A, x\mathbf{x}x, and b\mathbf{b}b.

Solution for Question 1

  1. Write the system in matrix form Ax=b\mathbf{A}\mathbf{x}=\mathbf{b}Ax=b.

[2−103−451−212400−321][x1x2x3x4]=[7−6101]\begin{bmatrix} 2 & -1 & 0 & 3 \\ -4 & 5 & 1 & -2 \\ 1 & 2 & 4 & 0 \\ 0 & -3 & 2 & 1 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} x_1\\x_2\\x_3\\x_4 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} 7\\-6\\10\\1 \end{bmatrix} ​2−410​−152−3​0142​3−201​​​x1​x2​x3​x4​​​=​7−6101​​

  1. Clearly identify A\mathbf{A}A, x\mathbf{x}x, and b\mathbf{b}b.

A=[2−103−451−212400−321],x=[x1x2x3x4],b=[7−6101]\mathbf{A}= \begin{bmatrix} 2 & -1 & 0 & 3 \\ -4 & 5 & 1 & -2 \\ 1 & 2 & 4 & 0 \\ 0 & -3 & 2 & 1 \end{bmatrix}, \quad \mathbf{x}= \begin{bmatrix} x_1\\x_2\\x_3\\x_4 \end{bmatrix}, \quad \mathbf{b}= \begin{bmatrix} 7\\-6\\10\\1 \end{bmatrix} A=​2−410​−152−3​0142​3−201​​,x=​x1​x2​x3​x4​​​,b=​7−6101​​

  1. State the dimensions of A\mathbf{A}A, x\mathbf{x}x, and b\mathbf{b}b.
  • A\mathbf{A}A is 4×44\times 44×4
  • x\mathbf{x}x is 4×14\times 14×1
  • b\mathbf{b}b is 4×14\times 14×1

Question 2 — 2D Linear Transformations (10 Points)

Let the vector

x=[12]\mathbf{x}= \begin{bmatrix} 1\\ 2 \end{bmatrix} x=[12​]

Consider the following three transformation matrices:

T1=[2001]T2=[0−330]T3=[1101]\mathbf{T}_1= \begin{bmatrix} 2 & 0\\ 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix} \qquad \mathbf{T}_2= \begin{bmatrix} 0 & -3\\ 3 & 0 \end{bmatrix} \qquad \mathbf{T}_3= \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 1\\ 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix} T1​=[20​01​]T2​=[03​−30​]T3​=[10​11​]

  1. Compute yk=Tkx\mathbf{y}_k=\mathbf{T}_k\mathbf{x}yk​=Tk​x for k=1,2,3k=1,2,3k=1,2,3.
  2. On a single set of axes, sketch the original vector x\mathbf{x}x and the three transformed vectors y1,y2,y3\mathbf{y}_1,\mathbf{y}_2,\mathbf{y}_3y1​,y2​,y3​.

Solution for Question 2

  1. Compute yk=Tkx\mathbf{y}_k=\mathbf{T}_k\mathbf{x}yk​=Tk​x for k=1,2,3k=1,2,3k=1,2,3.

y1=T1x=[2001][12]=[22]\mathbf{y}_1=\mathbf{T}_1\mathbf{x} = \begin{bmatrix} 2 & 0\\ 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} 1\\2 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} 2\\2 \end{bmatrix} y1​=T1​x=[20​01​][12​]=[22​]

y2=T2x=[0−330][12]=[−63]\mathbf{y}_2=\mathbf{T}_2\mathbf{x} = \begin{bmatrix} 0 & -3\\ 3 & 0 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} 1\\2 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} -6\\3 \end{bmatrix} y2​=T2​x=[03​−30​][12​]=[−63​]

y3=T3x=[1110][12]=[32]\mathbf{y}_3=\mathbf{T}_3\mathbf{x} = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 1\\ 1 & 0 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} 1\\2 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} 3\\2 \end{bmatrix} y3​=T3​x=[11​10​][12​]=[32​]

  1. On a single set of axes, sketch the original vector x\mathbf{x}x and the three transformed vectors y1,y2,y3\mathbf{y}_1,\mathbf{y}_2,\mathbf{y}_3y1​,y2​,y3​.

Question 3 — Matrix Multiplication (10 Points)

Let

A=[1−20341−12]B=[20−131−2450−3]\mathbf{A}= \begin{bmatrix} 1 & -2 \\ 0 & 3 \\ 4 & 1 \\ -1 & 2 \end{bmatrix} \qquad \mathbf{B}= \begin{bmatrix} 2 & 0 & -1 & 3 & 1 \\ -2 & 4 & 5 & 0 & -3 \end{bmatrix} A=​104−1​−2312​​B=[2−2​04​−15​30​1−3​]

  1. State the dimensions of A\mathbf{A}A, B\mathbf{B}B, and AB\mathbf{A}\mathbf{B}AB.
  2. Compute the product C=AB\mathbf{C}=\mathbf{A}\mathbf{B}C=AB.
  3. Show your work clearly for two entries of C\mathbf{C}C:
    • c11c_{11}c11​
    • c34c_{34}c34​

Solution for Question 3

  1. State the dimensions of A\mathbf{A}A, B\mathbf{B}B, and AB\mathbf{A}\mathbf{B}AB.
  • A\mathbf{A}A is 4×24\times 24×2
  • B\mathbf{B}B is 2×52\times 52×5
  • AB\mathbf{A}\mathbf{B}AB is 4×54\times 54×5
  1. Compute the product C=AB\mathbf{C}=\mathbf{A}\mathbf{B}C=AB.

C=AB=[1−20341−12][20−131−2450−3]=[6−8−1137−612150−9641121−6811−37]\mathbf{C}=\mathbf{A}\mathbf{B} = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & -2 \\ 0 & 3 \\ 4 & 1 \\ -1 & 2 \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} 2 & 0 & -1 & 3 & 1 \\ -2 & 4 & 5 & 0 & -3 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} 6 & -8 & -11 & 3 & 7 \\ -6 & 12 & 15 & 0 & -9 \\ 6 & 4 & 1 & 12 & 1\\ -6 & 8 & 11 & -3 & 7\\ \end{bmatrix} C=AB=​104−1​−2312​​[2−2​04​−15​30​1−3​]=​6−66−6​−81248​−1115111​3012−3​7−917​​

  1. Show your work clearly for two entries of C\mathbf{C}C:
    • c11=1∗2+(−2)∗(−2)=6c_{11} = 1 * 2 + (-2) * (-2) = 6c11​=1∗2+(−2)∗(−2)=6
    • c34=4∗3+1∗0=12c_{34} = 4 * 3 + 1 * 0 = 12c34​=4∗3+1∗0=12

Question 4 — Block Matrix Multiplication (10 Points)

Consider the matrices

K∈R6×6X∈R6×3\mathbf{K}\in\mathbb{R}^{6\times 6} \qquad \mathbf{X}\in\mathbb{R}^{6\times 3} K∈R6×6X∈R6×3

Matrix K\mathbf{K}K is partitioned into four blocks:

  • K11∈R4×4\mathbf{K}_{11}\in\mathbb{R}^{4\times 4}K11​∈R4×4
  • K12∈R4×2\mathbf{K}_{12}\in\mathbb{R}^{4\times 2}K12​∈R4×2
  • K21∈R2×4\mathbf{K}_{21}\in\mathbb{R}^{2\times 4}K21​∈R2×4
  • K22∈R2×2\mathbf{K}_{22}\in\mathbb{R}^{2\times 2}K22​∈R2×2

and matrix X\mathbf{X}X is partitioned compatibly as:

  • X1∈R4×3\mathbf{X}_1\in\mathbb{R}^{4\times 3}X1​∈R4×3
  • X2∈R2×3\mathbf{X}_2\in\mathbb{R}^{2\times 3}X2​∈R2×3

  1. Sketch out the partitioning on the matrices (you dont need to write out all the entries). Write the partitioned forms of K\mathbf{K}K and X\mathbf{X}X using block notation,

  2. Write the block multiplication expression for

    Y=KX\mathbf{Y}=\mathbf{K}\mathbf{X} Y=KX

    in terms of the submatrices.

  3. For each product appearing in your expression, state its dimensions. For example:

    K12X2:(4×2)(2×3)=(4×3)\mathbf{K}_{12}\mathbf{X}_2:\quad (4\times 2)(2\times 3)=(4\times 3) K12​X2​:(4×2)(2×3)=(4×3)

  4. State the final dimension of Y\mathbf{Y}Y.

You do not need to perform any numerical multiplication — the goal is to correctly set up the block multiplication and verify dimensional consistency.

Solution for Question 4

  1. Sketch out the partitioning on the matrices (you dont need to write out all the entries). Write the partitioned forms of K\mathbf{K}K and X\mathbf{X}X using block notation,

K=[k11k12k13k14k15k16k21k22k23k24k25k26k31k32k33k34k35k36k41k42k43k44k45k46k51k52k53k54k55k56k61k62k63k64k65k66]=[K11K12K21K22],K11∈R4×4,  K12∈R4×2,  K21∈R2×4,  K22∈R2×2.\mathbf{K}= \left[ \begin{array}{cccc|cc} k_{11} & k_{12} & k_{13} & k_{14} & k_{15} & k_{16} \\ k_{21} & k_{22} & k_{23} & k_{24} & k_{25} & k_{26} \\ k_{31} & k_{32} & k_{33} & k_{34} & k_{35} & k_{36} \\ k_{41} & k_{42} & k_{43} & k_{44} & k_{45} & k_{46} \\ \hline k_{51} & k_{52} & k_{53} & k_{54} & k_{55} & k_{56} \\ k_{61} & k_{62} & k_{63} & k_{64} & k_{65} & k_{66} \\ \end{array} \right] = \begin{bmatrix} \mathbf{K}_{11} & \mathbf{K}_{12}\\ \mathbf{K}_{21} & \mathbf{K}_{22} \end{bmatrix}, \quad \mathbf{K}_{11}\in\mathbb{R}^{4\times4},\; \mathbf{K}_{12}\in\mathbb{R}^{4\times2},\; \mathbf{K}_{21}\in\mathbb{R}^{2\times4},\; \mathbf{K}_{22}\in\mathbb{R}^{2\times2}. K=​k11​k21​k31​k41​k51​k61​​k12​k22​k32​k42​k52​k62​​k13​k23​k33​k43​k53​k63​​k14​k24​k34​k44​k54​k64​​k15​k25​k35​k45​k55​k65​​k16​k26​k36​k46​k56​k66​​​​=[K11​K21​​K12​K22​​],K11​∈R4×4,K12​∈R4×2,K21​∈R2×4,K22​∈R2×2.

X=[x11x12x13x21x22x23x31x32x33x41x42x43x51x52x53x61x62x63]=X=[X1X2],X1∈R4×3,  X2∈R2×3.\mathbf{X}= \left[ \begin{array}{ccc} x_{11} & x_{12} & x_{13}\\ x_{21} & x_{22} & x_{23}\\ x_{31} & x_{32} & x_{33}\\ x_{41} & x_{42} & x_{43}\\ \hline x_{51} & x_{52} & x_{53}\\ x_{61} & x_{62} & x_{63} \end{array} \right] = \mathbf{X}= \begin{bmatrix} \mathbf{X}_1\\ \mathbf{X}_2 \end{bmatrix}, \quad \mathbf{X}_1\in\mathbb{R}^{4\times3},\; \mathbf{X}_2\in\mathbb{R}^{2\times3}. X=​x11​x21​x31​x41​x51​x61​​x12​x22​x32​x42​x52​x62​​x13​x23​x33​x43​x53​x63​​​​=X=[X1​X2​​],X1​∈R4×3,X2​∈R2×3.

  1. Write the block multiplication expression for

    Y=KX\mathbf{Y}=\mathbf{K}\mathbf{X} Y=KX

    in terms of the submatrices.

Y=KX=[K11K12K21K22][X1X2]=[K11X1+K12X2K21X1+K22X2].\mathbf{Y}=\mathbf{K}\mathbf{X} = \begin{bmatrix} \mathbf{K}_{11} & \mathbf{K}_{12}\\ \mathbf{K}_{21} & \mathbf{K}_{22} \end{bmatrix} \begin{bmatrix} \mathbf{X}_1\\ \mathbf{X}_2 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} \mathbf{K}_{11}\mathbf{X}_1+\mathbf{K}_{12}\mathbf{X}_2\\ \mathbf{K}_{21}\mathbf{X}_1+\mathbf{K}_{22}\mathbf{X}_2 \end{bmatrix}. Y=KX=[K11​K21​​K12​K22​​][X1​X2​​]=[K11​X1​+K12​X2​K21​X1​+K22​X2​​].

  1. For each product appearing in your expression, state its dimensions.

K11X1:(4×4)(4×3)=(4×3)\mathbf{K}_{11}\mathbf{X}_1:\quad (4\times 4)(4\times 3)=(4\times 3) K11​X1​:(4×4)(4×3)=(4×3)

K12X2:(4×2)(2×3)=(4×3)\mathbf{K}_{12}\mathbf{X}_2:\quad (4\times 2)(2\times 3)=(4\times 3) K12​X2​:(4×2)(2×3)=(4×3)

K21X1:(2×4)(4×3)=(2×3)\mathbf{K}_{21}\mathbf{X}_1:\quad (2\times 4)(4\times 3)=(2\times 3) K21​X1​:(2×4)(4×3)=(2×3)

K22X2:(2×2)(2×3)=(2×3)\mathbf{K}_{22}\mathbf{X}_2:\quad (2\times 2)(2\times 3)=(2\times 3) K22​X2​:(2×2)(2×3)=(2×3)

  1. State the final dimension of Y\mathbf{Y}Y.

Y:(6×6)(6×3)=(6×3)\mathbf{Y}:\quad (6\times 6)(6\times 3)=(6\times 3) Y:(6×6)(6×3)=(6×3)

Question 5 — Gauss–Jordan (10 Points)

Let

A=[2101131002411012]b=[1234]\mathbf{A}= \begin{bmatrix} 2 & 1 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 3 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 2 & 4 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 & 1 & 2 \end{bmatrix} \qquad \mathbf{b}= \begin{bmatrix} 1\\2\\3\\4 \end{bmatrix} A=​2101​1320​0141​1012​​b=​1234​​

  1. Form the augmented matrix [A ∣ I][\mathbf{A}\,|\,\mathbf{I}][A∣I].
  2. Perform Gauss–Jordan elimination only through the first two pivots (i.e., make the first two pivot columns match I\mathbf{I}I).
  3. Clearly show your row operations and the partially reduced matrix.

Solution for Question 5

  1. Form the augmented matrix [A ∣ I][\mathbf{A}\,|\,\mathbf{I}][A∣I].

[A∣I]=[21011000131001000241001010120001]\mathbf{[A|I]}= \left[ \begin{array}{cccc|cccc} 2 & 1 & 0 & 1 & 1 & 0 & 0 & 0\\ 1 & 3 & 1 & 0 & 0 & 1 & 0 & 0\\ 0 & 2 & 4 & 1 & 0 & 0 & 1 & 0\\ 1 & 0 & 1 & 2 & 0 & 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array} \right] [A∣I]=​2101​1320​0141​1012​1000​0100​0010​0001​​

  1. Perform Gauss–Jordan elimination only through the first two pivots (i.e., make the first two pivot columns match I\mathbf{I}I).

  2. Clearly show your row operations and the partially reduced matrix.

Pivot 1 (column 1): Row operation:

  • R1←12R1R_1 \leftarrow \frac{1}{2}R_1R1​←21​R1​

[10.500.50.5000131001000241001010120001]\left[ \begin{array}{cccc|cccc} 1 & 0.5 & 0 & 0.5 & 0.5 & 0 & 0 & 0\\ 1 & 3 & 1 & 0 & 0 & 1 & 0 & 0\\ 0 & 2 & 4 & 1 & 0 & 0 & 1 & 0\\ 1 & 0 & 1 & 2 & 0 & 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array} \right] ​1101​0.5320​0141​0.5012​0.5000​0100​0010​0001​​

  • R2←R2−R1R_2 \leftarrow R_2-R_1R2​←R2​−R1​
  • R4←R4−R1R_4 \leftarrow R_4-R_1R4​←R4​−R1​

    [10.500.50.500002.51−0.5−0.5100024100100−0.511.5−0.5001]\left[ \begin{array}{cccc|cccc} 1 & 0.5 & 0 & 0.5 & 0.5 & 0 & 0 & 0\\ 0 & 2.5 & 1 & -0.5 & -0.5 & 1 & 0 & 0\\ 0 & 2 & 4 & 1 & 0 & 0 & 1 & 0\\ 0 & -0.5 & 1 & 1.5 & -0.5 & 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array} \right] ​1000​0.52.52−0.5​0141​0.5−0.511.5​0.5−0.50−0.5​0100​0010​0001​​

Pivot 2 (column 2):

  • R2←12.5R2R_2 \leftarrow \frac{1}{2.5}R_2R2​←2.51​R2​

    [10.500.50.5000010.4−0.2−0.20.400024100100−0.511.5−0.5001]\left[ \begin{array}{cccc|cccc} 1 & 0.5 & 0 & 0.5 & 0.5 & 0 & 0 & 0\\ 0 & 1 & 0.4 & -0.2 & -0.2 & 0.4 & 0 & 0\\ 0 & 2 & 4 & 1 & 0 & 0 & 1 & 0\\ 0 & -0.5 & 1 & 1.5 & -0.5 & 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array} \right] ​1000​0.512−0.5​00.441​0.5−0.211.5​0.5−0.20−0.5​00.400​0010​0001​​

  • R1←R1−12R2R_1 \leftarrow R_1-\frac{1}{2}R_2R1​←R1​−21​R2​

  • R3←R3−2R2R_3 \leftarrow R_3-2R_2R3​←R3​−2R2​

  • R4←R4+12R2R_4 \leftarrow R_4+\frac{1}{2}R_2R4​←R4​+21​R2​

    [10−0.20.60.6−0.200010.4−0.2−0.20.400003.21.40.4−0.810001.21.4−0.60.201]\left[ \begin{array}{cccc|cccc} 1 & 0 & -0.2 & 0.6 & 0.6 & -0.2 & 0 & 0\\ 0 & 1 & 0.4 & -0.2 & -0.2 & 0.4 & 0 & 0\\ 0 & 0 & 3.2 & 1.4 & 0.4 & -0.8 & 1 & 0\\ 0 & 0 & 1.2 & 1.4 & -0.6 & 0.2 & 0 & 1 \end{array} \right] ​1000​0100​−0.20.43.21.2​0.6−0.21.41.4​0.6−0.20.4−0.6​−0.20.4−0.80.2​0010​0001​​

Question 6 — LU Decomposition (10 Points)

Using the same matrix A\mathbf{A}A from Question 5:

  1. Perform Gaussian elimination (no pivoting, i.e., swapping rows) to produce U\mathbf{U}U.
  2. Record the elimination multipliers to construct L\mathbf{L}L (unit diagonal).
  3. Verify your result by computing (by hand) the product LU\mathbf{L}\mathbf{U}LU and checking that it equals A\mathbf{A}A.

Solution for Question 6

  1. Perform Gaussian elimination (no pivoting, i.e., swapping rows) to produce U\mathbf{U}U.

Ccolumn 1:

Elimination multipliers

ℓ21=a21a11=12,ℓ31=a31a11=0,ℓ41=a41a11=12\ell_{21}=\frac{a_{21}}{a_{11}}=\frac{1}{2}, \qquad \ell_{31}=\frac{a_{31}}{a_{11}}=0, \qquad \ell_{41}=\frac{a_{41}}{a_{11}}=\frac{1}{2} ℓ21​=a11​a21​​=21​,ℓ31​=a11​a31​​=0,ℓ41​=a11​a41​​=21​

Row operations (update the matrix to start forming U\mathbf{U}U)

  • R2←R2−12R1R_2 \leftarrow R_2-\frac{1}{2}R_1R2​←R2​−21​R1​
  • R4←R4−12R1R_4 \leftarrow R_4-\frac{1}{2}R_1R4​←R4​−21​R1​

[210102.51−0.502410−0.511.5]\begin{bmatrix} 2 & 1 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & 2.5 & 1 & -0.5 \\ 0 & 2 & 4 & 1 \\ 0 & -0.5 & 1 & 1.5 \end{bmatrix} ​2000​12.52−0.5​0141​1−0.511.5​​

Ccolumn 2:

Elimination multipliers

ℓ32=a32′a22′=45,ℓ42=a42′a22′=−15\ell_{32}=\frac{a'_{32}}{a'_{22}}=\frac{4}{5}, \qquad \ell_{42}=\frac{a'_{42}}{a'_{22}}=-\frac{1}{5} ℓ32​=a22′​a32′​​=54​,ℓ42​=a22′​a42′​​=−51​

Row operations (update the matrix to start forming U\mathbf{U}U)

  • R3←R3−45R2R_3 \leftarrow R_3-\frac{4}{5}R_2R3​←R3​−54​R2​
  • R4←R4+15R2R_4 \leftarrow R_4+\frac{1}{5}R_2R4​←R4​+51​R2​

[210102.51−0.5003.21.4001.21.4]\begin{bmatrix} 2 & 1 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & 2.5 & 1 & -0.5 \\ 0 & 0 & 3.2 & 1.4 \\ 0 & 0 & 1.2 & 1.4 \end{bmatrix} ​2000​12.500​013.21.2​1−0.51.41.4​​

Ccolumn 3:

Elimination multipliers

ℓ43=a43′a33′=38\ell_{43}=\frac{a'_{43}}{a'_{33}}=\frac{3}{8} ℓ43​=a33′​a43′​​=83​

Row operations (update the matrix to start forming U\mathbf{U}U)

  • R4←R4−38R3R_4 \leftarrow R_4-\frac{3}{8}R_3R4​←R4​−83​R3​

[210102.51−0.5003.21.40000.875]\begin{bmatrix} 2 & 1 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & 2.5 & 1 & -0.5 \\ 0 & 0 & 3.2 & 1.4 \\ 0 & 0 & 0 & 0.875 \end{bmatrix} ​2000​12.500​013.20​1−0.51.40.875​​

  1. Record the elimination multipliers to construct L\mathbf{L}L (unit diagonal).

L=[1000ℓ21100ℓ31ℓ3210ℓ41ℓ42ℓ431]=[10000.510000.8100.5−0.20.3751]\mathbf{L}= \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 0 & 0 & 0 \\ \ell_{21} & 1 & 0 & 0 \\ \ell_{31} & \ell_{32} & 1 & 0 \\ \ell_{41} & \ell_{42} & \ell_{43} & 1 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 0 & 0 & 0 \\ 0.5 & 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0.8 & 1 & 0 \\ 0.5 & -0.2 & 0.375 & 1 \end{bmatrix} L=​1ℓ21​ℓ31​ℓ41​​01ℓ32​ℓ42​​001ℓ43​​0001​​=​10.500.5​010.8−0.2​0010.375​0001​​

  1. Verify your result by computing (by hand) the product LU\mathbf{L}\mathbf{U}LU and checking that it equals A\mathbf{A}A.

L=[10000.510000.8100.5−0.20.3751],U=[210102.51−0.5003.21.40000.875].\mathbf{L}= \begin{bmatrix} 1 & 0 & 0 & 0\\ 0.5 & 1 & 0 & 0\\ 0 & 0.8 & 1 & 0\\ 0.5 & -0.2 & 0.375 & 1 \end{bmatrix}, \qquad \mathbf{U}= \begin{bmatrix} 2 & 1 & 0 & 1\\ 0 & 2.5 & 1 & -0.5\\ 0 & 0 & 3.2 & 1.4\\ 0 & 0 & 0 & 0.875 \end{bmatrix}. L=​10.500.5​010.8−0.2​0010.375​0001​​,U=​2000​12.500​013.20​1−0.51.40.875​​.

Compute LU\mathbf{L}\mathbf{U}LU row-by-row is a linear combination of rows of U\mathbf{U}U:

  • Row 1:

[1,0,0,0]U=R1(U)=[2,1,0,1].[1,0,0,0]\mathbf{U}=\text{R}_1(\mathbf{U})=[2,1,0,1]. [1,0,0,0]U=R1​(U)=[2,1,0,1].

  • Row 2:

[0.5,1,0,0]U=0.5 R1(U)+R2(U)[0.5,1,0,0]\mathbf{U}=0.5\,\text{R}_1(\mathbf{U})+\text{R}_2(\mathbf{U}) [0.5,1,0,0]U=0.5R1​(U)+R2​(U)

=0.5[2,1,0,1]+[0,2.5,1,−0.5]=[1,3,1,0].=0.5[2,1,0,1]+[0,2.5,1,-0.5]=[1,3,1,0]. =0.5[2,1,0,1]+[0,2.5,1,−0.5]=[1,3,1,0].

  • Row 3:

[0,0.8,1,0]U=0.8 R2(U)+R3(U)[0,0.8,1,0]\mathbf{U}=0.8\,\text{R}_2(\mathbf{U})+\text{R}_3(\mathbf{U}) [0,0.8,1,0]U=0.8R2​(U)+R3​(U)

=0.8[0,2.5,1,−0.5]+[0,0,3.2,1.4]=[0,2,4,1].=0.8[0,2.5,1,-0.5]+[0,0,3.2,1.4]=[0,2,4,1]. =0.8[0,2.5,1,−0.5]+[0,0,3.2,1.4]=[0,2,4,1].

  • Row 4:

[0.5,−0.2,0.375,1]U=0.5 R1(U)−0.2 R2(U)+0.375 R3(U)+R4(U)[0.5,-0.2,0.375,1]\mathbf{U} =0.5\,\text{R}_1(\mathbf{U})-0.2\,\text{R}_2(\mathbf{U})+0.375\,\text{R}_3(\mathbf{U})+\text{R}_4(\mathbf{U}) [0.5,−0.2,0.375,1]U=0.5R1​(U)−0.2R2​(U)+0.375R3​(U)+R4​(U)

=0.5[2,1,0,1]−0.2[0,2.5,1,−0.5]+0.375[0,0,3.2,1.4]+[0,0,0,0.875]=0.5[2,1,0,1]-0.2[0,2.5,1,-0.5]+0.375[0,0,3.2,1.4]+[0,0,0,0.875] =0.5[2,1,0,1]−0.2[0,2.5,1,−0.5]+0.375[0,0,3.2,1.4]+[0,0,0,0.875]

=[1,0,1,2].=[1,0,1,2]. =[1,0,1,2].

Therefore,

LU=[2101131002411012]=A.\mathbf{L}\mathbf{U}= \begin{bmatrix} 2 & 1 & 0 & 1\\ 1 & 3 & 1 & 0\\ 0 & 2 & 4 & 1\\ 1 & 0 & 1 & 2 \end{bmatrix} =\mathbf{A}. LU=​2101​1320​0141​1012​​=A.

Question 7 — Cholesky Factorization (20 Points)

Let the symmetric positive definite matrix

K=[4100131001210012]\mathbf{K}= \begin{bmatrix} 4 & 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 1 & 3 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 2 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 & 2 \end{bmatrix} K=​4100​1310​0121​0012​​

  1. Compute the Cholesky factorization K=LLT\mathbf{K}=\mathbf{L}\mathbf{L}^TK=LLT.
  2. Solve Kx=b\mathbf{K}\mathbf{x}=\mathbf{b}Kx=b for each right-hand side:

b1=[1000]b2=[0100]b3=[1111]\mathbf{b}_{1}= \begin{bmatrix} 1\\0\\0\\0 \end{bmatrix} \qquad \mathbf{b}_{2}= \begin{bmatrix} 0\\1\\0\\0 \end{bmatrix} \qquad \mathbf{b}_{3}= \begin{bmatrix} 1\\1\\1\\1 \end{bmatrix} b1​=​1000​​b2​=​0100​​b3​=​1111​​

For each solve, show:

  • forward substitution Lb^=b\mathbf{L}\hat{\mathbf{b}}=\mathbf{b}Lb^=b
  • back substitution LTx=b^\mathbf{L}^T\mathbf{x}=\hat{\mathbf{b}}LTx=b^

Solution for Question 7

  1. Compute the Cholesky factorization K=LLT\mathbf{K}=\mathbf{L}\mathbf{L}^TK=LLT.

Let

L=[ℓ11000ℓ21ℓ2200ℓ31ℓ32ℓ330ℓ41ℓ42ℓ43ℓ44]\mathbf{L}= \begin{bmatrix} \ell_{11}&0&0&0\\ \ell_{21}&\ell_{22}&0&0\\ \ell_{31}&\ell_{32}&\ell_{33}&0\\ \ell_{41}&\ell_{42}&\ell_{43}&\ell_{44} \end{bmatrix} L=​ℓ11​ℓ21​ℓ31​ℓ41​​0ℓ22​ℓ32​ℓ42​​00ℓ33​ℓ43​​000ℓ44​​​

Calculate using the Cholesky formula:

  • Column 1:

ℓ11=k11=4=2,ℓ21=k21ℓ11=12\ell_{11}=\sqrt{k_{11}}=\sqrt{4}=2,\qquad \ell_{21}=\frac{k_{21}}{\ell_{11}}=\frac{1}{2} ℓ11​=k11​​=4​=2,ℓ21​=ℓ11​k21​​=21​

ℓ31=k31ℓ11=0,ℓ41=k41ℓ11=0\ell_{31}=\frac{k_{31}}{\ell_{11}}=0,\qquad \ell_{41}=\frac{k_{41}}{\ell_{11}}=0 ℓ31​=ℓ11​k31​​=0,ℓ41​=ℓ11​k41​​=0

  • Column 2:

ℓ22=k22−ℓ212=3−(12)2=114=112\ell_{22}=\sqrt{k_{22}-\ell_{21}^2} =\sqrt{3-\left(\frac12\right)^2} =\sqrt{\frac{11}{4}} =\frac{\sqrt{11}}{2} ℓ22​=k22​−ℓ212​​=3−(21​)2​=411​​=211​​

ℓ32=k32−ℓ31ℓ21ℓ22=1−011/2=211=21111\ell_{32}=\frac{k_{32}-\ell_{31}\ell_{21}}{\ell_{22}} =\frac{1-0}{\sqrt{11}/2} =\frac{2}{\sqrt{11}} =\frac{2\sqrt{11}}{11} ℓ32​=ℓ22​k32​−ℓ31​ℓ21​​=11​/21−0​=11​2​=11211​​

ℓ42=k42−ℓ41ℓ21ℓ22=0−0ℓ22=0\ell_{42}=\frac{k_{42}-\ell_{41}\ell_{21}}{\ell_{22}} =\frac{0-0}{\ell_{22}}=0 ℓ42​=ℓ22​k42​−ℓ41​ℓ21​​=ℓ22​0−0​=0

  • Column 3:

ℓ33=k33−ℓ312−ℓ322=2−0−(21111)2=2−411=1811=32211\ell_{33}=\sqrt{k_{33}-\ell_{31}^2-\ell_{32}^2} =\sqrt{2-0-\left(\frac{2\sqrt{11}}{11}\right)^2} =\sqrt{2-\frac{4}{11}} =\sqrt{\frac{18}{11}} =\frac{3\sqrt{22}}{11} ℓ33​=k33​−ℓ312​−ℓ322​​=2−0−(11211​​)2​=2−114​​=1118​​=11322​​

ℓ43=k43−ℓ41ℓ31−ℓ42ℓ32ℓ33=1−0−0322/11=11322=226\ell_{43}=\frac{k_{43}-\ell_{41}\ell_{31}-\ell_{42}\ell_{32}}{\ell_{33}} =\frac{1-0-0}{3\sqrt{22}/11} =\frac{11}{3\sqrt{22}} =\frac{\sqrt{22}}{6} ℓ43​=ℓ33​k43​−ℓ41​ℓ31​−ℓ42​ℓ32​​=322​/111−0−0​=322​11​=622​​

  • Column 4:

ℓ44=k44−ℓ412−ℓ422−ℓ432=2−0−0−(226)2=2−2236=2518=526\ell_{44}=\sqrt{k_{44}-\ell_{41}^2-\ell_{42}^2-\ell_{43}^2} =\sqrt{2-0-0-\left(\frac{\sqrt{22}}{6}\right)^2} =\sqrt{2-\frac{22}{36}} =\sqrt{\frac{25}{18}} =\frac{5\sqrt{2}}{6} ℓ44​=k44​−ℓ412​−ℓ422​−ℓ432​​=2−0−0−(622​​)2​=2−3622​​=1825​​=652​​

Therefore:

L=[2000121120002111132211000226526]\mathbf{L}= \begin{bmatrix} 2&0&0&0\\ \frac12&\frac{\sqrt{11}}{2}&0&0\\ 0&\frac{2\sqrt{11}}{11}&\frac{3\sqrt{22}}{11}&0\\ 0&0&\frac{\sqrt{22}}{6}&\frac{5\sqrt{2}}{6} \end{bmatrix} L=​221​00​0211​​11211​​0​0011322​​622​​​000652​​​​

and K=LLT\mathbf{K}=\mathbf{L}\mathbf{L}^TK=LLT.

  1. Solve Kx=b\mathbf{K}\mathbf{x}=\mathbf{b}Kx=b for each right-hand side:

(a) b1=[1000]\mathbf{b}_1=\begin{bmatrix}1\\0\\0\\0\end{bmatrix}b1​=​1000​​

Forward substitution: Lb^=b1\mathbf{L}\hat{\mathbf{b}}=\mathbf{b}_1Lb^=b1​

Let:

b^=[y1y2y3y4]\hat{\mathbf{b}}=\begin{bmatrix}y_1\\y_2\\y_3\\y_4\end{bmatrix}b^=​y1​y2​y3​y4​​​

Then:

2y1=1⇒y1=122y_1=1 \Rightarrow y_1=\frac12 2y1​=1⇒y1​=21​

12y1+112y2=0⇒y2=−1122\frac12 y_1+\frac{\sqrt{11}}{2}y_2=0 \Rightarrow y_2=-\frac{\sqrt{11}}{22} 21​y1​+211​​y2​=0⇒y2​=−2211​​

21111y2+32211y3=0⇒y3=2266\frac{2\sqrt{11}}{11}y_2+\frac{3\sqrt{22}}{11}y_3=0 \Rightarrow y_3=\frac{\sqrt{22}}{66} 11211​​y2​+11322​​y3​=0⇒y3​=6622​​

226y3+526y4=0⇒y4=−230\frac{\sqrt{22}}{6}y_3+\frac{5\sqrt2}{6}y_4=0 \Rightarrow y_4=-\frac{\sqrt2}{30} 622​​y3​+652​​y4​=0⇒y4​=−302​​

b^1=[12−11222266−230]\hat{\mathbf{b}}_1= \begin{bmatrix} \frac12\\[2pt] -\frac{\sqrt{11}}{22}\\[2pt] \frac{\sqrt{22}}{66}\\[2pt] -\frac{\sqrt2}{30} \end{bmatrix} b^1​=​21​−2211​​6622​​−302​​​​

Back substitution: LTx=b^1\mathbf{L}^T\mathbf{x}=\hat{\mathbf{b}}_1LTx=b^1​

526x4=−230⇒x4=−125\frac{5\sqrt2}{6}x_4=-\frac{\sqrt2}{30} \Rightarrow x_4=-\frac{1}{25} 652​​x4​=−302​​⇒x4​=−251​

32211x3+226x4=2266⇒x3=225\frac{3\sqrt{22}}{11}x_3+\frac{\sqrt{22}}{6}x_4=\frac{\sqrt{22}}{66} \Rightarrow x_3=\frac{2}{25} 11322​​x3​+622​​x4​=6622​​⇒x3​=252​

112x2+21111x3=−1122⇒x2=−325\frac{\sqrt{11}}{2}x_2+\frac{2\sqrt{11}}{11}x_3=-\frac{\sqrt{11}}{22} \Rightarrow x_2=-\frac{3}{25} 211​​x2​+11211​​x3​=−2211​​⇒x2​=−253​

2x1+12x2=12⇒x1=7252x_1+\frac{1}{2}x_2=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow x_1=\frac{7}{25} 2x1​+21​x2​=21​⇒x1​=257​

x1=[725−325225−125]\mathbf{x}_1= \begin{bmatrix} \frac{7}{25}\\[2pt] -\frac{3}{25}\\[2pt] \frac{2}{25}\\[2pt] -\frac{1}{25} \end{bmatrix} x1​=​257​−253​252​−251​​​

(b) b2=[0100]\mathbf{b}_2=\begin{bmatrix}0\\1\\0\\0\end{bmatrix}b2​=​0100​​

Forward substitution: Lb^=b2\mathbf{L}\hat{\mathbf{b}}=\mathbf{b}_2Lb^=b2​

Let:

b^=[y1y2y3y4]\hat{\mathbf{b}}=\begin{bmatrix}y_1\\y_2\\y_3\\y_4\end{bmatrix}b^=​y1​y2​y3​y4​​​

Then:

2y1=0  ⇒  y1=02y_1=0 \;\Rightarrow\; y_1=0 2y1​=0⇒y1​=0

12y1+112y2=1  ⇒  y2=211=21111\frac12 y_1+\frac{\sqrt{11}}{2}y_2=1 \;\Rightarrow\; y_2=\frac{2}{\sqrt{11}}=\frac{2\sqrt{11}}{11} 21​y1​+211​​y2​=1⇒y2​=11​2​=11211​​

21111y2+32211y3=0  ⇒  y3=−22233\frac{2\sqrt{11}}{11}y_2+\frac{3\sqrt{22}}{11}y_3=0 \;\Rightarrow\; y_3=-\frac{2\sqrt{22}}{33} 11211​​y2​+11322​​y3​=0⇒y3​=−33222​​

226y3+526y4=0  ⇒  y4=2215\frac{\sqrt{22}}{6}y_3+\frac{5\sqrt{2}}{6}y_4=0 \;\Rightarrow\; y_4=\frac{2\sqrt{2}}{15} 622​​y3​+652​​y4​=0⇒y4​=1522​​

b^2=[021111−222332215]\hat{\mathbf{b}}_2= \begin{bmatrix} 0\\[2pt] \frac{2\sqrt{11}}{11}\\[2pt] -\frac{2\sqrt{22}}{33}\\[2pt] \frac{2\sqrt2}{15} \end{bmatrix} b^2​=​011211​​−33222​​1522​​​​

Back substitution: LTx=b^2\mathbf{L}^T\mathbf{x}=\hat{\mathbf{b}}_2LTx=b^2​

526x4=2215  ⇒  x4=425\frac{5\sqrt2}{6}x_4=\frac{2\sqrt2}{15} \;\Rightarrow\; x_4=\frac{4}{25} 652​​x4​=1522​​⇒x4​=254​

32211x3+226x4=−22233  ⇒  x3=−825\frac{3\sqrt{22}}{11}x_3+\frac{\sqrt{22}}{6}x_4=-\frac{2\sqrt{22}}{33} \;\Rightarrow\; x_3=-\frac{8}{25} 11322​​x3​+622​​x4​=−33222​​⇒x3​=−258​

112x2+21111x3=21111  ⇒  x2=1225\frac{\sqrt{11}}{2}x_2+\frac{2\sqrt{11}}{11}x_3=\frac{2\sqrt{11}}{11} \;\Rightarrow\; x_2=\frac{12}{25} 211​​x2​+11211​​x3​=11211​​⇒x2​=2512​

2x1+12x2=0  ⇒  x1=−3252x_1+\frac12 x_2=0 \;\Rightarrow\; x_1=-\frac{3}{25} 2x1​+21​x2​=0⇒x1​=−253​

x2=[−3251225−825425]\mathbf{x}_2= \begin{bmatrix} -\frac{3}{25}\\[2pt] \frac{12}{25}\\[2pt] -\frac{8}{25}\\[2pt] \frac{4}{25} \end{bmatrix} x2​=​−253​2512​−258​254​​​

(c) b3=[1111]\mathbf{b}_3=\begin{bmatrix}1\\1\\1\\1\end{bmatrix}b3​=​1111​​

Forward substitution: Lb^=b3\mathbf{L}\hat{\mathbf{b}}=\mathbf{b}_3Lb^=b3​

Let:

b^=[y1y2y3y4]\hat{\mathbf{b}}=\begin{bmatrix}y_1\\y_2\\y_3\\y_4\end{bmatrix}b^=​y1​y2​y3​y4​​​

Then:

2y1=1  ⇒  y1=122y_1=1 \;\Rightarrow\; y_1=\frac12 2y1​=1⇒y1​=21​

12y1+112y2=1  ⇒  y2=3211=31122\frac12 y_1+\frac{\sqrt{11}}{2}y_2=1 \;\Rightarrow\; y_2=\frac{3}{2\sqrt{11}}=\frac{3\sqrt{11}}{22} 21​y1​+211​​y2​=1⇒y2​=211​3​=22311​​

21111y2+32211y3=1  ⇒  y3=42233\frac{2\sqrt{11}}{11}y_2+\frac{3\sqrt{22}}{11}y_3=1 \;\Rightarrow\; y_3=\frac{4\sqrt{22}}{33} 11211​​y2​+11322​​y3​=1⇒y3​=33422​​

226y3+526y4=1  ⇒  y4=23\frac{\sqrt{22}}{6}y_3+\frac{5\sqrt{2}}{6}y_4=1 \;\Rightarrow\; y_4=\frac{\sqrt2}{3} 622​​y3​+652​​y4​=1⇒y4​=32​​

b^3=[12311224223323]\hat{\mathbf{b}}_3= \begin{bmatrix} \frac12\\[2pt] \frac{3\sqrt{11}}{22}\\[2pt] \frac{4\sqrt{22}}{33}\\[2pt] \frac{\sqrt2}{3} \end{bmatrix} b^3​=​21​22311​​33422​​32​​​​

Back substitution: LTx=b^3\mathbf{L}^T\mathbf{x}=\hat{\mathbf{b}}_3LTx=b^3​

526x4=23  ⇒  x4=25\frac{5\sqrt2}{6}x_4=\frac{\sqrt2}{3} \;\Rightarrow\; x_4=\frac{2}{5} 652​​x4​=32​​⇒x4​=52​

32211x3+226x4=42233  ⇒  x3=15\frac{3\sqrt{22}}{11}x_3+\frac{\sqrt{22}}{6}x_4=\frac{4\sqrt{22}}{33} \;\Rightarrow\; x_3=\frac{1}{5} 11322​​x3​+622​​x4​=33422​​⇒x3​=51​

112x2+21111x3=31122  ⇒  x2=15\frac{\sqrt{11}}{2}x_2+\frac{2\sqrt{11}}{11}x_3=\frac{3\sqrt{11}}{22} \;\Rightarrow\; x_2=\frac{1}{5} 211​​x2​+11211​​x3​=22311​​⇒x2​=51​

2x1+12x2=12  ⇒  x1=152x_1+\frac12 x_2=\frac12 \;\Rightarrow\; x_1=\frac{1}{5} 2x1​+21​x2​=21​⇒x1​=51​

x3=[15151525]\mathbf{x}_3= \begin{bmatrix} \frac15\\[2pt] \frac15\\[2pt] \frac15\\[2pt] \frac25 \end{bmatrix} x3​=​51​51​51​52​​​

Question 8 — Point Jacobi Iterations (20 Points)

Using the SPD system from Question 7 and b1\mathbf{b}_{1}b1​:

  1. Write the Point Jacobi update formula for the 4 unknowns.
  2. Perform two Jacobi iterations starting from

    x(0)=[0000]\mathbf{x}^{(0)} = \begin{bmatrix} 0\\ 0\\ 0\\ 0 \end{bmatrix} x(0)=​0000​​

  3. Perform two Jacobi iterations starting from

    x(0)=12x∗=12[x1∗x2∗x3∗x4∗]\mathbf{x}^{(0)} = \frac{1}{2}\mathbf{x}^\ast = \frac{1}{2} \begin{bmatrix} x_1^\ast\\ x_2^\ast\\ x_3^\ast\\ x_4^\ast \end{bmatrix} x(0)=21​x∗=21​​x1∗​x2∗​x3∗​x4∗​​​

    where x∗\mathbf{x}^\astx∗ is your solution from Question 7 for b1\mathbf{b}_{1}b1​.
  4. For each starting guess, report the residual norm

    ∥Kx(m)−b(1)∥2\|\mathbf{K}\mathbf{x}^{(m)}-\mathbf{b}^{(1)}\|_2 ∥Kx(m)−b(1)∥2​

    after each iteration (m=1m=1m=1 and m=2m=2m=2) (you may approximate to 2–3 decimals).

Solution for Question8

  1. Write the Point Jacobi update formula for the 4 unknowns.

x1(m)=b1−a12x2(m−1)−a13x3(m−1)−a14x4(m−1)a11x_1^{(m)} = \frac{b_1-a_{12}x_2^{(m-1)}-a_{13}x_3^{(m-1)}-a_{14}x_4^{(m-1)}}{a_{11}} x1(m)​=a11​b1​−a12​x2(m−1)​−a13​x3(m−1)​−a14​x4(m−1)​​

x2(m)=b2−a21x1(m−1)−a23x3(m−1)−a24x4(m−1)a22x_2^{(m)} = \frac{b_2-a_{21}x_1^{(m-1)}-a_{23}x_3^{(m-1)}-a_{24}x_4^{(m-1)}}{a_{22}} x2(m)​=a22​b2​−a21​x1(m−1)​−a23​x3(m−1)​−a24​x4(m−1)​​

x3(m)=b3−a31x1(m−1)−a32x2(m−1)−a34x4(m−1)a33x_3^{(m)} = \frac{b_3-a_{31}x_1^{(m-1)}-a_{32}x_2^{(m-1)}-a_{34}x_4^{(m-1)}}{a_{33}} x3(m)​=a33​b3​−a31​x1(m−1)​−a32​x2(m−1)​−a34​x4(m−1)​​

x4(m)=b4−a41x1(m−1)−a42x2(m−1)−a43x3(m−1)a44x_4^{(m)} = \frac{b_4-a_{41}x_1^{(m-1)}-a_{42}x_2^{(m-1)}-a_{43}x_3^{(m-1)}}{a_{44}} x4(m)​=a44​b4​−a41​x1(m−1)​−a42​x2(m−1)​−a43​x3(m−1)​​

  1. Perform two Jacobi iterations starting from

    x(0)=[0000]\mathbf{x}^{(0)} = \begin{bmatrix} 0\\ 0\\ 0\\ 0 \end{bmatrix} x(0)=​0000​​

  • First Iteration

x1(1)=b1−a12x2(0)−a13x3(0)−a14x4(0)a11=1−0−0−04=14x_1^{(1)} = \frac{b_1-a_{12}x_2^{(0)}-a_{13}x_3^{(0)}-a_{14}x_4^{(0)}}{a_{11}} = \frac{1-0-0-0}{4} = \frac{1}{4} x1(1)​=a11​b1​−a12​x2(0)​−a13​x3(0)​−a14​x4(0)​​=41−0−0−0​=41​

x2(1)=b2−a21x1(0)−a23x3(0)−a24x4(0)a22=0−0−0−03=0x_2^{(1)} = \frac{b_2-a_{21}x_1^{(0)}-a_{23}x_3^{(0)}-a_{24}x_4^{(0)}}{a_{22}} = \frac{0-0-0-0}{3} =0 x2(1)​=a22​b2​−a21​x1(0)​−a23​x3(0)​−a24​x4(0)​​=30−0−0−0​=0

x3(1)=b3−a31x1(0)−a32x2(0)−a34x4(0)a33=0−0−0−02=0x_3^{(1)} = \frac{b_3-a_{31}x_1^{(0)}-a_{32}x_2^{(0)}-a_{34}x_4^{(0)}}{a_{33}} = \frac{0-0-0-0}{2} =0 x3(1)​=a33​b3​−a31​x1(0)​−a32​x2(0)​−a34​x4(0)​​=20−0−0−0​=0

x4(1)=b4−a41x1(0)−a42x2(0)−a43x3(0)a44=0−0−0−02=0x_4^{(1)} = \frac{b_4-a_{41}x_1^{(0)}-a_{42}x_2^{(0)}-a_{43}x_3^{(0)}}{a_{44}} = \frac{0-0-0-0}{2} =0 x4(1)​=a44​b4​−a41​x1(0)​−a42​x2(0)​−a43​x3(0)​​=20−0−0−0​=0

x(1)=[14000]\mathbf{x}^{(1)} = \begin{bmatrix} \frac{1}{4}\\[2pt] 0\\ 0\\ 0 \end{bmatrix} x(1)=​41​000​​

  • Second Iteration

x1(2)=b1−a12x2(1)−a13x3(1)−a14x4(1)a11=1−0−0−04=14x_1^{(2)} = \frac{b_1-a_{12}x_2^{(1)}-a_{13}x_3^{(1)}-a_{14}x_4^{(1)}}{a_{11}} = \frac{1-0-0-0}{4} = \frac{1}{4} x1(2)​=a11​b1​−a12​x2(1)​−a13​x3(1)​−a14​x4(1)​​=41−0−0−0​=41​

x2(2)=b2−a21x1(1)−a23x3(1)−a24x4(1)a22=0−14−0−03=−112x_2^{(2)} = \frac{b_2-a_{21}x_1^{(1)}-a_{23}x_3^{(1)}-a_{24}x_4^{(1)}}{a_{22}} = \frac{0-\frac{1}{4}-0-0}{3} =-\frac{1}{12} x2(2)​=a22​b2​−a21​x1(1)​−a23​x3(1)​−a24​x4(1)​​=30−41​−0−0​=−121​

x3(2)=b3−a31x1(1)−a32x2(1)−a34x4(1)a33=0−0−0−02=0x_3^{(2)} = \frac{b_3-a_{31}x_1^{(1)}-a_{32}x_2^{(1)}-a_{34}x_4^{(1)}}{a_{33}} = \frac{0-0-0-0}{2} =0 x3(2)​=a33​b3​−a31​x1(1)​−a32​x2(1)​−a34​x4(1)​​=20−0−0−0​=0

x4(2)=b4−a41x1(1)−a42x2(1)−a43x3(1)a44=0−0−0−02=0x_4^{(2)} = \frac{b_4-a_{41}x_1^{(1)}-a_{42}x_2^{(1)}-a_{43}x_3^{(1)}}{a_{44}} = \frac{0-0-0-0}{2} =0 x4(2)​=a44​b4​−a41​x1(1)​−a42​x2(1)​−a43​x3(1)​​=20−0−0−0​=0

x(2)=[14−11200]\mathbf{x}^{(2)} = \begin{bmatrix} \frac{1}{4}\\[2pt] -\frac{1}{12}\\[2pt] 0\\ 0 \end{bmatrix} x(2)=​41​−121​00​​

  1. Perform two Jacobi iterations starting from

    x(0)=12x∗=12[x1∗x2∗x3∗x4∗]\mathbf{x}^{(0)} = \frac{1}{2}\mathbf{x}^\ast = \frac{1}{2} \begin{bmatrix} x_1^\ast\\ x_2^\ast\\ x_3^\ast\\ x_4^\ast \end{bmatrix} x(0)=21​x∗=21​​x1∗​x2∗​x3∗​x4∗​​​

    where x∗\mathbf{x}^\astx∗ is your solution from Question 7 for b1\mathbf{b}_{1}b1​.

x(0)=12x∗=12[x1∗x2∗x3∗x4∗]=x1=[750−350250−150]\mathbf{x}^{(0)} = \frac{1}{2}\mathbf{x}^\ast = \frac{1}{2} \begin{bmatrix} x_1^\ast\\ x_2^\ast\\ x_3^\ast\\ x_4^\ast \end{bmatrix} = \mathbf{x}_1= \begin{bmatrix} \frac{7}{50}\\[2pt] -\frac{3}{50}\\[2pt] \frac{2}{50}\\[2pt] -\frac{1}{50} \end{bmatrix} x(0)=21​x∗=21​​x1∗​x2∗​x3∗​x4∗​​​=x1​=​507​−503​502​−501​​​

  • First Iteration

x1(1)=b1−a12x2(0)−a13x3(0)−a14x4(0)a11=1−(−350)−0−04=53200x_1^{(1)} = \frac{b_1-a_{12}x_2^{(0)}-a_{13}x_3^{(0)}-a_{14}x_4^{(0)}}{a_{11}} = \frac{1-(-\frac{3}{50})-0-0}{4} = \frac{53}{200} x1(1)​=a11​b1​−a12​x2(0)​−a13​x3(0)​−a14​x4(0)​​=41−(−503​)−0−0​=20053​

x2(1)=b2−a21x1(0)−a23x3(0)−a24x4(0)a22=0−750−125−03=−350x_2^{(1)} = \frac{b_2-a_{21}x_1^{(0)}-a_{23}x_3^{(0)}-a_{24}x_4^{(0)}}{a_{22}} = \frac{0-\frac{7}{50}-\frac{1}{25}-0}{3} =-\frac{3}{50} x2(1)​=a22​b2​−a21​x1(0)​−a23​x3(0)​−a24​x4(0)​​=30−507​−251​−0​=−503​

x3(1)=b3−a31x1(0)−a32x2(0)−a34x4(0)a33=0−(−350)−(−150)−02=125x_3^{(1)} = \frac{b_3-a_{31}x_1^{(0)}-a_{32}x_2^{(0)}-a_{34}x_4^{(0)}}{a_{33}} = \frac{0-(-\frac{3}{50})-(-\frac{1}{50})-0}{2} =\frac{1}{25} x3(1)​=a33​b3​−a31​x1(0)​−a32​x2(0)​−a34​x4(0)​​=20−(−503​)−(−501​)−0​=251​

x4(1)=b4−a41x1(0)−a42x2(0)−a43x3(0)a44=0−0−0−1252=−150x_4^{(1)} = \frac{b_4-a_{41}x_1^{(0)}-a_{42}x_2^{(0)}-a_{43}x_3^{(0)}}{a_{44}} = \frac{0-0-0-\frac{1}{25}}{2} =-\frac{1}{50} x4(1)​=a44​b4​−a41​x1(0)​−a42​x2(0)​−a43​x3(0)​​=20−0−0−251​​=−501​

x(1)=[53200−350125−150]\mathbf{x}^{(1)} = \begin{bmatrix} \frac{53}{200}\\[2pt] -\frac{3}{50}\\[2pt] \frac{1}{25}\\[2pt] -\frac{1}{50} \end{bmatrix} x(1)=​20053​−503​251​−501​​​

  • Second Iteration

x1(2)=b1−a12x2(1)−a13x3(1)−a14x4(1)a11=1−(−350)−0−04=53200x_1^{(2)} = \frac{b_1-a_{12}x_2^{(1)}-a_{13}x_3^{(1)}-a_{14}x_4^{(1)}}{a_{11}} = \frac{1-(-\frac{3}{50})-0-0}{4} = \frac{53}{200} x1(2)​=a11​b1​−a12​x2(1)​−a13​x3(1)​−a14​x4(1)​​=41−(−503​)−0−0​=20053​

x2(2)=b2−a21x1(1)−a23x3(1)−a24x4(1)a22=0−53200−125−03=−61600x_2^{(2)} = \frac{b_2-a_{21}x_1^{(1)}-a_{23}x_3^{(1)}-a_{24}x_4^{(1)}}{a_{22}} = \frac{0-\frac{53}{200}-\frac{1}{25}-0}{3} =-\frac{61}{600} x2(2)​=a22​b2​−a21​x1(1)​−a23​x3(1)​−a24​x4(1)​​=30−20053​−251​−0​=−60061​

x3(2)=b3−a31x1(1)−a32x2(1)−a34x4(1)a33=0−(−350)−(−150)−02=125x_3^{(2)} = \frac{b_3-a_{31}x_1^{(1)}-a_{32}x_2^{(1)}-a_{34}x_4^{(1)}}{a_{33}} = \frac{0-(-\frac{3}{50})-(-\frac{1}{50})-0}{2} =\frac{1}{25} x3(2)​=a33​b3​−a31​x1(1)​−a32​x2(1)​−a34​x4(1)​​=20−(−503​)−(−501​)−0​=251​

x4(2)=b4−a41x1(1)−a42x2(1)−a43x3(1)a44=0−0−0−1252=−150x_4^{(2)} = \frac{b_4-a_{41}x_1^{(1)}-a_{42}x_2^{(1)}-a_{43}x_3^{(1)}}{a_{44}} = \frac{0-0-0-\frac{1}{25}}{2} =-\frac{1}{50} x4(2)​=a44​b4​−a41​x1(1)​−a42​x2(1)​−a43​x3(1)​​=20−0−0−251​​=−501​

x(2)=[53200−61600125−150]\mathbf{x}^{(2)} = \begin{bmatrix} \frac{53}{200}\\[2pt] -\frac{61}{600}\\[2pt] \frac{1}{25}\\[2pt] -\frac{1}{50} \end{bmatrix} x(2)=​20053​−60061​251​−501​​​

  1. For each starting guess, report the residual norm

    ∥Kx(m)−b(1)∥2\|\mathbf{K}\mathbf{x}^{(m)}-\mathbf{b}^{(1)}\|_2 ∥Kx(m)−b(1)∥2​

    after each iteration (m=1m=1m=1 and m=2m=2m=2) (you may approximate to 2–3 decimals).

4. Residual norms for each starting guess (using results from Q2 and Q3)

We report the Euclidean residual norm:

∥Kx(m)−b(1)∥2,\|Kx^{(m)}-b^{(1)}\|_2, ∥Kx(m)−b(1)∥2​,

where

K=[4100131001210012],b(1)=[1000].K=\begin{bmatrix} 4&1&0&0\\ 1&3&1&0\\ 0&1&2&1\\ 0&0&1&2 \end{bmatrix},\qquad b^{(1)}=\begin{bmatrix}1\\0\\0\\0\end{bmatrix}. K=​4100​1310​0121​0012​​,b(1)=​1000​​.

  • Case A (from Q2): start with x(0)=[0000]x^{(0)}=\begin{bmatrix}0\\0\\0\\0\end{bmatrix}x(0)=​0000​​

Residual at m=1m=1m=1:

Kx(1)=[4×14+1×01×14+3×0+1×01×0+2×0+1×01×0+2×0]=[11400]Kx^{(1)}= \begin{bmatrix} 4\times\frac14+1\times 0\\ 1\times\frac14+3\times 0+1\times 0\\ 1\times 0+2\times 0+1\times 0\\ 1\times 0+2\times 0 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} 1\\\frac14\\0\\0 \end{bmatrix} Kx(1)=​4×41​+1×01×41​+3×0+1×01×0+2×0+1×01×0+2×0​​=​141​00​​

r(1)=Kx(1)−b(1)=[01400]r^{(1)}=Kx^{(1)}-b^{(1)}= \begin{bmatrix} 0\\\frac14\\0\\0 \end{bmatrix} r(1)=Kx(1)−b(1)=​041​00​​

∥r(1)∥2=14\|r^{(1)}\|_2=\frac14 ∥r(1)∥2​=41​

Residual at m=2m=2m=2:

Kx(2)=[4×14+1×(−112)1×14+3×(−112)+1×01×(−112)+2×0+1×01×0+2×0]=[11120−1120]Kx^{(2)}= \begin{bmatrix} 4\times\frac14+1\times\left(-\frac{1}{12}\right)\\ 1\times\frac14+3\times\left(-\frac{1}{12}\right)+1\times 0\\ 1\times\left(-\frac{1}{12}\right)+2\times 0+1\times 0\\ 1\times 0+2\times 0 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} \frac{11}{12}\\ 0\\ -\frac{1}{12}\\ 0 \end{bmatrix} Kx(2)=​4×41​+1×(−121​)1×41​+3×(−121​)+1×01×(−121​)+2×0+1×01×0+2×0​​=​1211​0−121​0​​

r(2)=Kx(2)−b(1)=[−1120−1120]r^{(2)}=Kx^{(2)}-b^{(1)}= \begin{bmatrix} -\frac{1}{12}\\ 0\\ -\frac{1}{12}\\ 0 \end{bmatrix} r(2)=Kx(2)−b(1)=​−121​0−121​0​​

∥r(2)∥2=(112)2+(112)2=212\|r^{(2)}\|_2=\sqrt{\left(\frac{1}{12}\right)^2+\left(\frac{1}{12}\right)^2} =\frac{\sqrt2}{12} ∥r(2)∥2​=(121​)2+(121​)2​=122​​

  • Case B (from Q3): start with x(0)=12x∗x^{(0)}=\frac12 x^*x(0)=21​x∗

From Q3:

x(1)=[53200−350125−150],x(2)=[53200−61600125−150]x^{(1)}=\begin{bmatrix} \frac{53}{200}\\[2pt] -\frac{3}{50}\\[2pt] \frac{1}{25}\\[2pt] -\frac{1}{50} \end{bmatrix},\qquad x^{(2)}=\begin{bmatrix} \frac{53}{200}\\[2pt] -\frac{61}{600}\\[2pt] \frac{1}{25}\\[2pt] -\frac{1}{50} \end{bmatrix} x(1)=​20053​−503​251​−501​​​,x(2)=​20053​−60061​251​−501​​​

Residual at m=1m=1m=1:

Kx(1)=[4×53200+1×(−350)1×53200+3×(−350)+1×1251×(−350)+2×125+1×(−150)1×125+2×(−150)]=[11800]Kx^{(1)}= \begin{bmatrix} 4\times\frac{53}{200}+1\times\left(-\frac{3}{50}\right)\\ 1\times\frac{53}{200}+3\times\left(-\frac{3}{50}\right)+1\times\frac{1}{25}\\ 1\times\left(-\frac{3}{50}\right)+2\times\frac{1}{25}+1\times\left(-\frac{1}{50}\right)\\ 1\times\frac{1}{25}+2\times\left(-\frac{1}{50}\right) \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} 1\\ \frac{1}{8}\\ 0\\ 0 \end{bmatrix} Kx(1)=​4×20053​+1×(−503​)1×20053​+3×(−503​)+1×251​1×(−503​)+2×251​+1×(−501​)1×251​+2×(−501​)​​=​181​00​​

r(1)=Kx(1)−b(1)=[01800],∥r(1)∥2=18r^{(1)}=Kx^{(1)}-b^{(1)}= \begin{bmatrix} 0\\\frac{1}{8}\\0\\0 \end{bmatrix}, \qquad \|r^{(1)}\|_2=\frac{1}{8} r(1)=Kx(1)−b(1)=​081​00​​,∥r(1)∥2​=81​

Residual at m=2m=2m=2:

Kx(2)=[4×53200+1×(−61600)1×53200+3×(−61600)+1×1251×(−61600)+2×125+1×(−150)1×125+2×(−150)]=[5756000−256000]Kx^{(2)}= \begin{bmatrix} 4\times\frac{53}{200}+1\times\left(-\frac{61}{600}\right)\\ 1\times\frac{53}{200}+3\times\left(-\frac{61}{600}\right)+1\times\frac{1}{25}\\ 1\times\left(-\frac{61}{600}\right)+2\times\frac{1}{25}+1\times\left(-\frac{1}{50}\right)\\ 1\times\frac{1}{25}+2\times\left(-\frac{1}{50}\right) \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} \frac{575}{600}\\ 0\\ -\frac{25}{600}\\ 0 \end{bmatrix} Kx(2)=​4×20053​+1×(−60061​)1×20053​+3×(−60061​)+1×251​1×(−60061​)+2×251​+1×(−501​)1×251​+2×(−501​)​​=​600575​0−60025​0​​

r(2)=Kx(2)−b(1)=[−256000−256000]=[−1240−1240]r^{(2)}=Kx^{(2)}-b^{(1)}= \begin{bmatrix} -\frac{25}{600}\\ 0\\ -\frac{25}{600}\\ 0 \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} -\frac{1}{24}\\ 0\\ -\frac{1}{24}\\ 0 \end{bmatrix} r(2)=Kx(2)−b(1)=​−60025​0−60025​0​​=​−241​0−241​0​​

∥r(2)∥2=(124)2+(124)2=224\|r^{(2)}\|_2=\sqrt{\left(\frac{1}{24}\right)^2+\left(\frac{1}{24}\right)^2} =\frac{\sqrt{2}}{24} ∥r(2)∥2​=(241​)2+(241​)2​=242​​

  • Summary

Start with x(0)=0x^{(0)}=\mathbf{0}x(0)=0:

∥Kx(1)−b(1)∥2=14,∥Kx(2)−b(1)∥2=212.\|Kx^{(1)}-b^{(1)}\|_2=\frac14,\qquad \|Kx^{(2)}-b^{(1)}\|_2=\frac{\sqrt2}{12}. ∥Kx(1)−b(1)∥2​=41​,∥Kx(2)−b(1)∥2​=122​​.

Start with x(0)=12x∗x^{(0)}=\frac12 x^*x(0)=21​x∗:

∥Kx(1)−b(1)∥2=18,∥Kx(2)−b(1)∥2=224.\|Kx^{(1)}-b^{(1)}\|_2=\frac{1}{8},\qquad \|Kx^{(2)}-b^{(1)}\|_2=\frac{\sqrt{2}}{24}. ∥Kx(1)−b(1)∥2​=81​,∥Kx(2)−b(1)∥2​=242​​.
